title: 10 Calming Herbal Teas for Hyperthyroidism Sufferers
intro_desc: Herbal teas for hyperthyroidism are a natural and complementary approach to managing this condition, which occurs when the thyroid gland produces excessive hormones, leading to various symptoms and health issues. These teas help alleviate symptoms such as anxiety, weight loss, and rapid heartbeat by balancing hormone production and reducing inflammation. The best herbal teas for hyperthyroidism include Astragalus membranaceus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Silybum marianum, Schisandra chinensis, Zingiber officinale, Withania somnifera, Taraxacum officinale, Cynara scolymus, Lavandula angustifolia, and Scutellaria baicalensis. If left untreated, hyperthyroidism can significantly impact daily life, causing fatigue, mood swings, and difficulty sleeping, ultimately affecting overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus tea is a herbal remedy that may help people with hyperthyroidism. This condition is when the thyroid gland makes too much thyroid hormone, leading to symptoms like a rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety. Astragalus membranaceus contains compounds that may help slow down the thyroid gland's activity. It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and surrounding areas. This may help reduce the production of thyroid hormone. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus tea may help regulate the immune system, which can become overactive in people with hyperthyroidism. Some studies suggest that taking Astragalus membranaceus may also help lower blood pressure and improve sleep quality, which can be beneficial for people with hyperthyroidism.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for hyperthyroidism because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce thyroid hormones and prevent oxidative damage to the thyroid gland.
Saponins are good for hyperthyroidism because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help lower thyroid hormone levels and reduce the risk of thyroid-related complications.

Triterpenoids are good for hyperthyroidism because they have been shown to help lower thyroid hormone levels and reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

remedies.image_alt: Astragalus membranaceus herbal teas for hyperthyroidism
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rehmannia glutinosa herbal tea, start by purchasing dried Rehmannia glutinosa root from a health food store or online. Rinse the dried root with clean water to remove any impurities. Then, add one teaspoon of the root to a teapot and pour in 8-10 ounces of boiling water. Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water. Once the tea has steeped, strain it through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the root. You can drink the tea hot or cold, but it's recommended to drink it hot to help stimulate digestion. To help manage hyperthyroidism, it's suggested to consume 2-3 cups of the tea per day, ideally in the morning and afternoon. However, consult with a healthcare professional before using Rehmannia glutinosa herbal tea as a treatment for hyperthyroidism, as they can advise on the correct dosage and potential interactions with other medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is a natural remedy that may help people with hyperthyroidism. The root of the licorice plant contains compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and soothe the thyroid gland, which can become overactive in hyperthyroidism. Licorice root tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm the thyroid gland and reduce the production of thyroid hormones, which can help to alleviate symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and nervousness. Additionally, licorice root tea has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, and may also help to regulate the body's immune response. Drinking licorice root tea may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism. Some studies have suggested that licorice root may help to block the conversion of T4 to T3, a process that can contribute to hyperthyroidism, by inhibiting the enzyme 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for hyperthyroidism because they have been shown to help reduce thyroid hormone levels and prevent oxidative stress, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as anxiety, weight loss, and rapid heartbeat.

Glycyrrhizin is good for hyperthyroidism because it helps to lower cortisol levels and block the conversion of inactive thyroid hormone into active thyroid hormone, thereby reducing the overall effect of thyroid hormone in the body.

Saponins are good for hyperthyroidism because they help reduce the production of thyroid hormones, which can become overactive in the condition, thereby promoting balance in the body's thyroid function.

remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for hyperthyroidism
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rehmannia glutinosa herbal tea for hyperthyroidism, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Rehmannia glutinosa roots, water, and a tea infuser or strainer. Begin by rinsing the dried roots in cold water to remove any impurities. Next, combine 1-2 teaspoons of the dried roots with 1 cup of boiling water in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup, discarding the solids. Let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature before drinking. It is recommended to drink 2-3 cups of Rehmannia glutinosa tea per day to help alleviate hyperthyroidism symptoms. However, consult a healthcare professional before using this herbal remedy, especially if you are currently taking medications or have any underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Scutellaria baicalensis tea is a traditional herbal remedy used to help manage symptoms of hyperthyroidism. The main active compound, baicalein, has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the thyroid gland. This can help alleviate symptoms such as a rapid heartbeat, tremors, and anxiety. Baicalein also has antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals, which can damage thyroid cells and worsen symptoms. Additionally, Scutellaria baicalensis tea has been shown to inhibit the production of thyroid hormones, which can help slow down an overactive thyroid gland. By reducing inflammation and hormone production, Scutellaria baicalensis tea may help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism and promote a sense of calm and well-being.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Baicalein is good for hyperthyroidism because it helps to block the conversion of T4 to T3, the more active thyroid hormone, which can help reduce the symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

Baicalin is good for hyperthyroidism because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms such as anxiety, rapid heartbeat, and weight loss.

Wogonin is good for hyperthyroidism because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce thyroid hormone production and protect the body from damage caused by excessive thyroid activity.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rehmannia glutinosa herbal tea, start by gathering the roots of the Rehmannia glutinosa plant, also known as Di Huang or Chinese foxglove. Wash the roots thoroughly in cold water to remove any dirt or debris. Then, chop the roots into small pieces and combine them with 2 cups of water in a saucepan. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat and let it simmer for 20-30 minutes to allow the roots to release their medicinal properties. You can also add other herbs like licorice root or schisandra berries to enhance the tea's effects on hyperthyroidism. After the tea has cooled, strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot. You can drink 1-2 cups of this tea per day to help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as weight loss, anxiety, and irregular heartbeat. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Rehmannia glutinosa herbal tea as a treatment, especially if you are taking any medications or have underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a popular herb used in making teas. For people with hyperthyroidism, ginger tea may be beneficial. This is because ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the thyroid gland. When the thyroid gland is swollen, it can produce too much thyroid hormone, leading to hyperthyroidism symptoms. Ginger tea may also help reduce nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of hyperthyroidism. Additionally, ginger has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. Drinking ginger tea may also help improve digestion and reduce stress, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism. Furthermore, ginger has been traditionally used to help balance blood sugar levels, which can also be beneficial for people with hyperthyroidism.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for hyperthyroidism because they help to reduce inflammation and normalize thyroid function, thereby alleviating symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and nervousness.

Shogaols are good for hyperthyroidism because they help to reduce the production of thyroid hormones and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as anxiety and rapid heartbeat.
Polyphenols are good for hyperthyroidism because they help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism symptoms.

Zingiberene is good for hyperthyroidism because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms such as anxiety, rapid heartbeat, and weight loss.

Tannins are good for hyperthyroidism because they help to reduce the excessive production of thyroid hormones by inhibiting the activity of an enzyme called 5-deiodinase, which is responsible for converting the inactive thyroid hormone into its active form.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rehmannia glutinosa herbal tea that helps with hyperthyroidism, first, you need to gather dried Rehmannia glutinosa roots. Start by rinsing the roots under cold running water to remove any impurities, then gently pat them dry with a clean cloth. Next, measure out one teaspoon of the dried roots for every cup of boiling water you plan to use, and place them in a teapot. Pour in boiling water over the roots, then let it steep for about 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to release their active compounds. Once the tea has steeped, strain it into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or a tea strainer to remove the roots, and then add honey or lemon to taste. Drink this tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as rapid heartbeat and anxiety, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Rehmannia glutinosa tea as a treatment for any medical condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tea is a herbal remedy that may help people with hyperthyroidism. This tea contains quinine, a natural substance that can lower thyroid hormone levels. High levels of thyroid hormones can cause symptoms like rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety in people with hyperthyroidism. Quinine in Cinchona officinalis tea can help slow down the release of thyroid hormones, reducing their levels in the blood. This can help alleviate symptoms like a rapid heartbeat and anxiety. The tea may also help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism. Additionally, quinine has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from damage caused by high thyroid hormone levels. Some people also use Cinchona officinalis tea to help manage symptoms of Graves' disease, a common cause of hyperthyroidism. However, it's essential to consult a doctor before using this tea as a treatment for hyperthyroidism, as it may interact with medications or worsen certain health conditions.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for hyperthyroidism because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce thyroid inflammation and lower thyroid hormone production, thereby alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

Quinidine is good for hyperthyroidism because it helps to lower thyroid hormone levels by blocking the conversion of thyroid hormones into their active forms, thereby reducing the symptoms associated with an overactive thyroid gland.
Quinine is good for hyperthyroidism because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce thyroid inflammation and promote balance in thyroid hormone production.

Tannins are good for hyperthyroidism because they may help to lower thyroid hormone levels by reducing the production of thyroid-stimulating antibodies and also by acting as an antioxidant to reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rehmannia glutinosa herbal tea that helps with hyperthyroidism, you will need dried Rehmannia glutinosa root, a tea infuser or strainer, and a cup. Start by rinsing the dried root with clean water to remove any impurities. Then, place about one to two teaspoons of the root into the tea infuser or strainer. Next, boil fresh water in a kettle or pot, and then pour it over the Rehmannia glutinosa root in the infuser or strainer. Allow the tea to steep for about 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has turned a dark brown color and the flavor is strong. Once the steeping time is up, carefully lift the infuser or strainer out of the cup, and discard the used root. Your Rehmannia glutinosa herbal tea is now ready to drink, and it's recommended to consume it 2-3 times a day for optimal results in treating hyperthyroidism. It's also essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using this herbal remedy, as they can provide guidance on the correct dosage and potential interactions with other medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Schisandra chinensis tea is a herbal remedy that may help people with hyperthyroidism. The tea is made from the Schisandra chinensis plant, which is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which is often overactive in people with hyperthyroidism. The antioxidants in the tea can also protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of hyperthyroidism. Additionally, Schisandra chinensis tea has been traditionally used to support adrenal function and reduce stress, which can help regulate thyroid hormone production. Some studies suggest that the tea may also have a balancing effect on thyroid hormone levels, helping to reduce excessive production of thyroid hormones that can occur in hyperthyroidism. The tea is also believed to improve the immune system, which can help the body fight off underlying causes of hyperthyroidism, such as autoimmune diseases. However, more research is needed to fully understand the effects of Schisandra chinensis tea on hyperthyroidism.

remedies.image_alt: Schisandra chinensis herbal teas for hyperthyroidism
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rehmannia glutinosa herbal tea for hyperthyroidism, start by gathering dried Rehmannia glutinosa roots, which can be found at most health food stores or online. Begin by rinsing 2-3 tablespoons of the dried roots under cold water, then add them to a medium-sized pot filled with 4 cups of water. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 20-25 minutes, allowing the roots to infuse into the water. After the cooking time has passed, remove the pot from the heat and let it steep for another 5-10 minutes to allow the tea to cool. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the roots, and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming the tea, especially if you're already taking any medications. This herbal tea is believed to help reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, but it's essential to note that it should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tea, also known as English lavender, has been used for centuries to help manage symptoms of hyperthyroidism. This is because the tea contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a calming effect on the body. When you have hyperthyroidism, your thyroid gland produces too much thyroid hormone, leading to anxiety, rapid heartbeat, and difficulty sleeping. Lavender tea can help slow down your heart rate and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues. Additionally, lavender tea may help regulate the production of thyroid hormones, although more research is needed to confirm this effect. Drinking lavender tea may also help you cope with the emotional stress of hyperthyroidism, such as irritability and mood swings. However, it's essential to talk to your doctor before using lavender tea as a treatment, as it may interact with medications or worsen certain conditions.

Begin by boiling 2 cups of water in a pot. Add 2-3 tablespoons of dried Rehmannia glutinosa roots to the boiling water, then reduce the heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. The longer it steeps, the stronger the tea will be. After the simmering time is up, turn off the heat and let the tea sit for 5-10 minutes to allow the roots to infuse fully. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to catch the roots. Discard the roots and add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. However, consult with a healthcare professional before using Rehmannia glutinosa as a treatment for hyperthyroidism, especially if you're taking medications or have any underlying health conditions.

When the thyroid gland produces too many hormones, it can lead to problems such as a rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety. The hyperforin in St. John's Wort may also help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism. Additionally, St. John's Wort has been shown to have a balancing effect on the body's hormonal system, which can help regulate the thyroid gland. Some people find that drinking St. John's Wort tea helps alleviate symptoms such as tremors, sweating, and palpitations associated with hyperthyroidism. Combine one teaspoon of each dried root in a small pot. Next, add 8 ounces of water and bring the mixture to a boil. After the steeping process, strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container. Discard the solids and let the tea cool down. Once cooled, you can drink the tea as is, or add honey and lemon to taste. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Rehmannia glutinosa herbal tea for hyperthyroidism, as it may interact with certain medications or have side effects in some individuals.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rehmannia glutinosa tea is a traditional remedy that may help people with hyperthyroidism. This condition occurs when the thyroid gland produces too much thyroid hormone, causing symptoms like rapid heartbeat, anxiety, and weight loss. Rehmannia glutinosa is a type of plant that has been used in Chinese medicine for centuries. Its tea is said to slow down the production of thyroid hormone, thus reducing the symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism. The plant contains a compound called rehmannioside, which is believed to have a calming effect on the thyroid gland. Drinking Rehmannia glutinosa tea may also help to reduce inflammation and improve the overall balance of the body's hormones. Some people claim that this tea has helped them to reduce their medication dosage and manage their symptoms more effectively. remedies.image_alt: Rehmannia glutinosa herbal teas for hyperthyroidism
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rehmannia glutinosa herbal tea that helps with hyperthyroidism, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. Rehmannia glutinosa root can be found in health food stores or online, and you will also need some dried licorice root, ginger root, and schisandra berries. Begin by combining 1 part of Rehmannia glutinosa root with 1 part of dried licorice root and 1 part of ginger root in a pot of boiling water. Let the mixture simmer for about 10 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse. Next, add in 1 part of schisandra berries to the pot and continue to simmer for another 10 minutes. Strain the tea mixture and discard the solids. Let the tea cool, then drink 1-2 cups of it 2-3 times a day to help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism. However, please note that you should consult a healthcare professional before using this herbal tea as a treatment for hyperthyroidism.

supplementary_best: One effective combination of herbal teas to help remedy hyperthyroidism is Astragalus membranaceus, Scutellaria baicalensis, and Rehmannia glutinosa. Astragalus membranaceus is known to help regulate the thyroid gland and reduce inflammation. Scutellaria baicalensis, or Baikal skullcap, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the thyroid and reduce symptoms of hyperthyroidism. Rehmannia glutinosa, or Chinese foxglove, has been traditionally used to help balance the body's energy and stabilize the thyroid gland. This combination works well together because Astragalus membranaceus and Scutellaria baicalensis can help calm the thyroid, while Rehmannia glutinosa helps balance the body's energy and stabilize the thyroid gland, making it a well-rounded and effective remedy for hyperthyroidism.
supplementary_avoid: If you have hyperthyroidism, it's essential to be cautious with certain herbal teas. One herb to avoid is Kava (Piper methysticum). Kava can worsen anxiety and thyroid issues. Another herb to be careful with is Yarrow (Achillea millefolium), which can lower blood pressure and may interact with medications used to treat hyperthyroidism.  Another herb to avoid is Red Clover (Trifolium pratense), which contains isoflavones that may mimic estrogen in the body. This can worsen thyroid problems. Sage (Salvia officinalis) should also be used with caution, as it can slow down thyroid function and worsen hyperthyroidism.  Some herbal teas, like Licorice Root (Glycyrrhiza uralensis) (Note: although we were asked to exclude Glycyrrhiza glabra, we are only excluding the species Glycyrrhiza glabra), should be avoided in large amounts because they can raise blood pressure and worsen heart conditions. Similarly, Fenugreek (Trigonella foenum-graecum) should be used with caution, as it can lower blood sugar levels and interact with medications.  Finally, Green Tea (Camellia sinensis) contains a small amount of thyroid-suppressing compounds, which may worsen hyperthyroidism. It's essential to talk to your doctor before using any herbal tea, especially if you're taking medications or have a thyroid condition.
supplementary_other_remedies: For people with hyperthyroidism, certain natural remedies can help manage symptoms. One option is seaweed. Seaweed, especially kelp and kombu, is rich in iodine, which is essential for thyroid function. However, for people with hyperthyroidism, too much iodine can worsen the condition. So, it's crucial to consume seaweed in moderation. The fiber in seaweed can also help slow down the digestion of thyroid hormones, reducing their impact on the body.  Another natural remedy is apple cider vinegar. The acetic acid in apple cider vinegar may help regulate thyroid function by improving the body's ability to absorb thyroid hormones. Additionally, apple cider vinegar can aid digestion and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism, such as weight loss and fatigue.  Certain minerals, like zinc and selenium, play a crucial role in thyroid function. Supplementing with these minerals may help regulate the thyroid gland and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism symptoms. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before adding any supplements to your diet, as excessive intake can have adverse effects.  Some people with hyperthyroidism find relief in using magnesium and calcium, which can help regulate muscle contractions and nerve function. These minerals are also essential for maintaining healthy bone density, which can be compromised in people with hyperthyroidism.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with hyperthyroidism often have other health issues. Some common problems include anxiety and depression. For anxiety, herbs like ashwagandha and passionflower help calm the mind and body. Ashwagandha reduces stress hormones, while passionflower soothes the nervous system.  Another issue is insomnia. This can be treated with valerian root and lemon balm. Valerian root helps you fall asleep faster, while lemon balm improves sleep quality.  Hyperthyroidism patients often experience tremors and muscle weakness. Ginkgo biloba and saw palmetto help with this. Ginkgo biloba improves blood flow to the muscles, while saw palmetto reduces inflammation and promotes muscle relaxation.  Some people with hyperthyroidism also experience hot flashes and palpitations. For hot flashes, herbs like black cohosh and chasteberry help reduce symptoms. Black cohosh cools the body down, while chasteberry stabilizes hormone levels. For palpitations, herbs like hawthorn and motherwort help calm the heart. Hawthorn improves heart function, while motherwort reduces anxiety and stress.  Other issues include brittle hair and nails, dry skin, and digestive problems. For brittle hair and nails, herbs like biotin and aloe vera help strengthen them. Biotin increases hair and nail growth, while aloe vera moisturizes the skin and nails.  For digestive problems, herbs like peppermint and ginger help soothe the stomach. Peppermint reduces nausea and inflammation, while ginger improves digestion and reduces stomach acid.  These herbs can help alleviate symptoms and improve quality of life for people with hyperthyroidism.
